nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q1.alt: Question 1. Read paragraphs 3 and 4 from the story. “Wear it around your neck,” he whispered, “and you’ll understand everything that animals say. But if you reveal your secret, the charm won’t work anymore.” “I won’t tell anyone,” Niel assured him. Which word from paragraph 3 or 4 best helps the reader understand the meaning of the word “reveal”? Choices: A: whispered B: understand C: tell D: assured

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q2.alt: Question 2. Why does Niel clasp his charm in paragraph 8? Choices: A: He was afraid he would lose the charm. B: He could understand what the mouse was saying. C: He wanted to make sure the charm was still working. D: He could understand how the snake felt about secrets.

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q3.alt: Question 3. How does Niel add to the problem in this story? Choices: A: He invites his aunt to come visit. B: He laughs at what the animals say. C: He gives the snake tooth to his wife. D: He helps eat all the food in the house.

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q4.alt: Question 4. Which paragraph best explains the powers the charm has? Choices: A: paragraph 3 B: paragraph 5 C: paragraph 28 D: paragraph 30

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q5.alt: Question 5. Read paragraph 31 from the story. The snake rattled his tail and laughed. “I told you that secrets are hard to keep, my friend. We’ll see how long you last this time.” Why does the snake say this to Niel? Choices: A: The snake thinks the secret is funny. B: The snake thinks Niel will tell someone the secret. C: The snake thinks the wife will figure out the secret. D: The snake thinks his friendship with Niel is more valuable than secrets.

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q6.alt: Question 6. Which statement best describes a lesson learned from this story? Choices: A: Secrets can often be entertaining. B: Secrets are not very useful. C: Rewards should not be taken for granted. D: Rewards can be more trouble than they are worth.

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q7.alt: Question 7. Based on paragraphs 2 and 7, a “paleontologist” is a person who mainly Choices: A: digs deep in the ground B: studies ancient animal bones C: leads a big team D: finds complete animal skeletons

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q8.alt: Question 8. Read this sentence from paragraph 7. He added, “You know, Ben, if you’re tired of searching, you can always help someone else on the crew.” What does this show about Dave? Choices: A: He is concerned that Ben may be feeling unhappy. B: He hopes that Ben will work harder than he has been. C: He needs Ben to work with some of the others in the group. D: He is worried that Ben will become careless with the tools.

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q9.alt: Question 9. Paragraphs 9 through 11 show that Benny Choices: A: knows about changes that took place over time B: prefers working in cooler weather C: enjoys thinking out loud D: continues to work hard

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q10.alt: Question 10. Which paragraph shows a change in Benny’s feelings? Choices: A: paragraph 4 B: paragraph 8 C: paragraph 9 D: paragraph 12

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q11.alt: Question 11. How is the dig different from what Benny thought it would be? Choices: A: Benny uses brushes rather than shovels. B: Benny did not expect the land to be so hilly. C: Benny did not expect to find so many workers. D: Benny has to help the assistants.

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q12.alt: Question 12. Based on the article, what do all the young animals have in common? Choices: A: They play in groups with other animals. B: They need to prepare for when they are older. C: They are able to locate items that interest them. D: They let other animals know that they want to play.

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q13.alt: Question 13. Which definition matches the meaning of “frolic” as it is used in paragraph 3? Choices: A: to run around B: to find protection C: to learn by watching D: to walk next to its mother

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q14.alt: Question 14. The word “stalks” in paragraph 4 shows that the lion cub Choices: A: follows silently B: moves quickly C: looks around D: bends down

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q15.alt: Question 15. Paragraphs 3 through 5 support a main idea because they show Choices: A: how animals are different B: how animals escape trouble C: what animals need to learn D: that animals help each other

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q16.alt: Question 16. Before wolf pups can hunt together, they must first Choices: A: discover new games B: learn to use signals C: practice with toys D: know how to race

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q17.alt: Question 17. Read this sentence from paragraph 9. If one youngster plays too rough, the others will let him know they are unhappy with his behavior. What does this sentence show about the animals? Choices: A: The older animals introduce the games to the younger ones. B: The larger animals are prevented from wrestling with the smaller ones. C: The young animals enforce limits so that their games are safe for all. D: The adult animals are nearby to make sure that no one gets hurt.

nysed-ela-2015-g3-mc-q18.alt: Question 18. Which sentence best states a main idea of the entire article? Choices: A: “For animals in the wild, play is important to their very survival.” (paragraph 2) B: “Much like human children, young dolphins love to play with toys.” (paragraph 8) C: “When they play, the young animals learn to keep their footing no matter what might happen.” (paragraph 10) D: “Playing makes them strong and confident.” (paragraph 12)
